text_excerpt: Latest News - Darren Paffey MP Skip to main content Join Get Involved Donate About My story My Missions for Southampton Itchen. My Campaigns Have your say on £20m funding for Weston Bring a Neighbourhood Health Centre to Bitterne Bring Back Local Banking! Step-Free Access at Woolston Train Station More Bobbies on the Beat End the Cladding & Leasehold Scandal Bathing Water Status for the River Itchen Latest News Parliamentary Activity Get in Touch Contact Me Invite Darren to Your Event Upcoming Events SEND Parent & Carer Coffee Morning Town Hall Q&A with Darren Paffey MP Advice Surgeries Pint with your MP Southampton Action Forums Newsletter Sign Up More Search Search the website Search Menu Latest News Latest News from Darren Paffey MP See more Stronger protections for Southampton’s renters from today A fairer deal for renters and landlords in the city. Press Release 01 May 2026 The end of a bumper Parliamentary session Some reflections on nearly two years delivering change for Southampton. Press Release 30 April 2026 You’re invited to my online discussion about SEND reforms Another opportunity to have your say on wide-ranging reforms to SEND education. Press Release 27 April 2026 Help shape a better future for Weston A new neighbourhood board will decide on priorities for £20m investment in the area. Press Release 24 April 2026 I’ve written to the culture secretary about protecting a free press Bullying legal tactics must not be allowed to prevent legitimate public interest journalism. Press Release 20 April 2026 You’re invited to my SEND coffee morning An opportunity to share your views on wide-ranging reforms to SEND education. Press Release 02 April 2026 £42 million boost for local jobs and skills A new MoD contract will create new jobs and apprenticeship opportunities in Southampton. Press Release 31 March 2026 I’ve helped secure major road upgrades in Southampton A transformative investment for the city's road users. Press Release 26 March 2026 Funding for more new nurseries in Southampton More children in the city will get access to affordable childcare closer to home.
